frame_analyses.description: Red foam training pad with concentric rectangular grooves visible, no instruments yet present
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Standard FLS task 5 setup with foam pad

frame_analyses.description: Two needle drivers visible entering field, black suture material present, beginning needle loading
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appropriate instrument entry, suture appears to be correct material

frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver grasping needle, left instrument assisting with suture positioning
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good bimanual coordination during needle loading phase

frame_analyses.description: Needle being positioned for first penetration through foam pad
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle appears properly loaded at appropriate distance from tip

frame_analyses.description: Needle passing through foam pad, right needle driver driving needle through material
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean needle passage, appropriate entry angle

frame_analyses.description: Beginning knot formation, suture being manipulated for first throw
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Transition to knot tying phase, instruments working together

frame_analyses.description: First throw formation in progress, right hand leading the throw
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appears to be forming surgeon's knot (double throw) as required

frame_analyses.description: Second throw being formed, hands appear to have switched roles
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper hand switching technique observed between throws

frame_analyses.description: Final throw formation, completing the knot sequence
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Completing required three-throw sequence with alternating hands

frame_analyses.description: Task appears complete, suture ends being cut
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appropriate suture cutting to complete task

knot_assessments.notes: Proper hand switching and single throw technique
Final throw completed with proper hand switching

confidence_rationale: This appears to be a demonstration/training video rather than an actual task performance. The frames show technique examples and annotations rather than a continuous performance. Specific penalty measurements cannot be accurately determined from these demonstration frames.
cannot_determine: Exact suture placement accuracy relative to marks - demonstration format obscures target marks
Actual completion time - appears to be edited demonstration rather than real-time performance
Precise knot security - demonstration format prevents detailed knot assessment
technique_summary: This appears to be an instructional demonstration of Task 5 technique rather than an actual scored performance. The frames show proper technique elements including needle loading, suture placement, and three-throw knot sequence with hand switching.
strengths: Proper demonstration of surgeon's knot formation
Clear illustration of hand switching between throws
improvement_suggestions: Cannot provide specific improvement suggestions as this appears to be a demonstration video rather than a performance to be scored
